title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 16th April 2026 || TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Prefers Ather over Ola as of now
recommendations.detail: Asked Ola vs Ather (viewer Surya), Kutumba Rao's preference is Ather as of now. Ola may see a short-term move but Ather looks fundamentally better. On Ola, wait for results before gaining confidence since management always speaks bullishly.

recommendations.note: Long-term dividend play; hold, don't panic-sell
recommendations.detail: Viewer Sheshasai bought at Rs 449. Buy/hold only with a 2-5 year outlook; if you panic over a 10-20 rupee fall, don't touch it. It is a dividend play plus some capital-appreciation expectation, suited to low-risk-appetite investors. It rallied strongly and pulled back with volatility but will slowly recover - no worry if 5-10% down. Hold for the long term, not for short-term moves.

recommendations.note: Good company; hold, watch under-ownership factor
recommendations.detail: Viewer Satish Rao is at a ~37% loss. Kutumba Rao says it is fundamentally a good company; don't keep over-high price expectations. The key factor now is under-ownership raised by recent weakness - under-owned stocks rise quickly as fresh buying comes in. It's a good bet.

recommendations.note: Good company; hold, watch under-ownership factor
recommendations.detail: Viewer Satish Rao is at a ~45% loss on Tata Motors (passenger). Kutumba Rao says fundamentally good; don't set too-high price expectations. Look at the under-ownership factor since under-owned stocks rise quickly on fresh buying; otherwise fundamentals are equal. A good bet.

recommendations.note: Good company; hold, watch under-ownership factor
recommendations.detail: Part of viewer Satish Rao's basket sitting at 25-40% losses. Kutumba Rao views these as fundamentally good companies; keep modest expectations and focus on the under-ownership factor that drives quick recoveries. A good bet.
